title: How to build a JEPA and SSL on Audio/Acoustics
subtitle: A BirdCLEF 2026 experiment showcase for predictive acoustic representation learning

case_study.name: BirdCLEF++ / BirdCLEF 2026
case_study.task: Identify wildlife species from passive acoustic monitoring recordings collected across the Pantanal wetlands.

objectives: Frame acoustic self-supervised learning as representation learning under limited labels.
Explain the core JEPA idea: predict latent target representations from context, not raw samples.
Map audio waveforms to log-mel spectrogram patches suitable for masked predictive learning.
Sketch a small JEPA-style pretraining loop and downstream species classifier.
Discuss what changes when moving from a demo to BirdCLEF-scale field audio.
